summary: Completed comprehensive testing of FactuYa! invoicing app with subscription model. Backend APIs (15/15 tests passed), frontend flows tested including registration, login, invoice creation, subscription dialog, and Stripe checkout redirect.

critical.issue: Returns 403 'No subscription found' for new users who haven't called /api/subscription/status first. Subscription should be created during user registration.

minor.issue: Creates subscription lazily on first call instead of during registration. This causes invoice creation to fail for new users until they explicitly check subscription status.

integration_issues.issue: New users get 403 error when trying to create first invoice because subscription isn't initialized. The frontend shows subscription dialog but displays '0 of 10 invoices used' which is confusing.

issues: Console warnings about HTML nesting: <tr> cannot be child of <span>, <span> cannot be child of <tbody>. This is a React hydration issue in the invoice preview table.

Console warnings about HTML nesting:
cannot be descendant of

,

cannot be descendant of

. AlertDialogDescription renders as

but contains

and

children.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Create subscription during user registration in /api/auth/register endpoint, not lazily in /api/subscription/status
FIX (MEDIUM): Fix HTML nesting issues in InvoicePreview.jsx - wrap table row mapping properly
FIX (MEDIUM): Fix HTML nesting in SubscriptionDialog.jsx - AlertDialogDescription should not contain block elements
critical_code_review_comments: subscription_check.py returns 'No subscription found' when subscription doesn't exist - should auto-create trial subscription instead of failing
InvoicePreview.jsx line 110-112: Table rows are wrapped in a span which causes hydration errors

SubscriptionDialog.jsx line 72-83: AlertDialogDescription (renders as

) contains

and

elements which is invalid HTML

success_rate.frontend: 90% (all flows work but with console warnings)

test_credentials: Any email/password can be used to register new test users
seed_data_creation: Test users created during testing with pattern: test_*@example.com
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs work correctly. Main issue is subscription not being created during registration - must call /api/subscription/status before creating invoices. Stripe checkout integration works correctly with emergentintegrations library. Delete invoice dialog was not fully tested due to subscription initialization issue.

features_tested.user_registration: PASS - Users can register and are redirected to dashboard
features_tested.user_login: PASS - Users can login with valid credentials
features_tested.subscription_status: PASS - Returns correct trial info (10 free invoices)
features_tested.invoice_creation: PASS (with workaround) - Works after subscription is initialized
features_tested.subscription_limit_403: PASS - API returns 403 when 10 invoices reached
features_tested.stripe_checkout_redirect: PASS - Subscribe button redirects to Stripe checkout
features_tested.payment_return_handling: NOT FULLY TESTED - Dashboard has code to handle payment=success params
features_tested.delete_invoice_dialog: NOT TESTED - Could not test due to subscription initialization issue
features_tested.invoice_crud: PASS (backend) - All CRUD operations work via API

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Subscription is created lazily in /api/subscription/status endpoint (lines 46-61 in subscription.py) instead of during user registration. The check_can_create_invoice function in subscription_check.py returns False with 'No subscription found' when subscription doesn't exist.
reproduction_steps: 2. Immediately try to create an invoice via /api/invoices POST
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Either: (1) Create subscription during user registration in auth.py, or (2) Modify check_can_create_invoice to auto-create trial subscription if none exists
